title: A New Year
excerpt: The calendar changed, but my habit of looking for your face in every crowd remained exactly the same.

title: The First Text
excerpt: I typed out 'Happy New Year' and stared at it for an hour before deleting it. I didn't want to be just another notification.

title: Shared Smiles
excerpt: You smiled at a joke I made. For the rest of the week, I replayed that three-second memory.

title: The Wrong Name
excerpt: You called me by someone else's name by accident. It was a small slip, but it reminded me of my place.

title: Late Mornings
excerpt: Waking up at 11 AM meant the day was shorter, but the hours spent missing you felt just as endless.

title: The Coffee Order
excerpt: I memorized your coffee order perfectly, hoping one day you'd ask me to grab it for you.

title: Silent Observers
excerpt: Everyone in the room was looking at the presentation. I was only looking at your reflection in the glass.

title: The Blue Jacket
excerpt: You wore a blue jacket today. Suddenly, blue became my absolute favorite color in the world.

title: Unseen Effort
excerpt: I dressed a little better when I knew you'd be around, but your eyes always looked right past me.

title: The Question
excerpt: You asked me for the time. It was the only time our voices connected all week.

title: Cinematic Frame
excerpt: If my life was a cinematic vlog, you were the beautiful scenery I could only admire from a distance.

title: The Accident
excerpt: Our hands brushed by accident when reaching for the same pen. I felt electricity; you felt nothing.

title: Eve of Romance
excerpt: The world prepared for Valentine's Day. I prepared to watch you receive flowers from someone else.

title: The Day After
excerpt: You told me about the perfect date you had. I smiled and nodded, swallowing a bitter pill.

title: The Umbrella
excerpt: It rained, and I offered you my umbrella. I walked home completely soaked, but perfectly happy.

title: The Spreadsheet
excerpt: I stared at the Microsoft Excel cells on my screen, wishing there was a formula to calculate how to make you notice me.

title: A Borrowed Pen
excerpt: You borrowed my pen and returned it. I kept it separate from all the others because you touched it.

title: The Whisper
excerpt: You whispered a secret to me. For a moment, I felt like I was the most important person in your world.

title: The Laugh
excerpt: Your laugh echoed down the hallway. I didn't even need to see you to know your eyes were crinkling.

title: The Group Photo
excerpt: In the group photo, I made sure to stand right behind you, just so we would be close in the picture.

title: Warehouse Aisles
excerpt: I walked the long aisles of the warehouse, feeling like a misplaced item that wasn't on your list.

title: The Recommendation
excerpt: You recommended a movie. I watched it three times that same night just to understand your taste.

title: The Ignore
excerpt: You walked past me without saying hello. It was a busy day for you, but it ruined mine.

title: Hindi Melodies
excerpt: That old Hindi song played in the background, speaking the exact words my heart was too afraid to say out loud.

title: The Unread Book
excerpt: I bought the book you were reading just so we would have something to talk about. You finished it before I could start.

title: The Dream
excerpt: I woke up from a dream where you loved me back. Reality felt like a harsh punishment.

title: The Typo
excerpt: You texted me with a typo, and I spent twenty minutes analyzing if it had a hidden meaning.

title: The Distance
excerpt: We sat right next to each other, but the silence between us stretched for miles.

title: The Overheard Conversation
excerpt: I heard you talking about your ideal type. I mentally checked my traits and failed every criteria.

title: The High Resolution
excerpt: I rendered my memories of you in 4K, vivid and sharp, while my own presence faded into your background.

title: The Missed Call
excerpt: My phone rang and my heart jumped hoping it was you. It was just a wrong number.

title: The Favorite Spot
excerpt: I started sitting at the table near the window, only because I knew it was your favorite spot.

title: Bengali Verses
excerpt: A beautiful Bengali poem reminded me of your eyes, deep and entirely oblivious to my existence.

title: The Apology
excerpt: I apologized for bumping into you, just to have a reason to hear you say 'it's okay'.

title: The Advice
excerpt: You asked me what I thought of his new haircut. I lied and said it looked great on him.

title: The Late Shift
excerpt: Working the late shift was exhausting, but seeing you leave the building made the fatigue disappear.

title: The Shared Look
excerpt: Someone dropped a glass, and we made eye contact and smiled. A split second of a shared universe.

title: The Ocean Waves
excerpt: I thought of you by the beach, knowing my hopes would eventually be washed away just like footprints in the sand.

title: The Empty Hallway
excerpt: The hallway felt incredibly empty when you weren't walking down it.

title: The Compliment
excerpt: You told me my shirt looked nice. I made sure to wear it every single Tuesday after that.

title: The Unsent Letter
excerpt: I wrote a letter explaining everything. It's still sitting at the bottom of my top drawer.

title: The Rainy Window
excerpt: I watched the rain slide down the glass, tracing the drops just like I traced your profile in my mind.

title: Assamese Rhythms
excerpt: The beat of the Assamese song was uplifting, but my heart dragged slowly, stuck in a rhythm only meant for one.

title: The Name Drop
excerpt: Hearing someone else say your name makes my head snap up instinctively.

title: The Birthday
excerpt: I bought you a gift but signed the card from 'the team' so you wouldn't think I was weird.

title: The Rejection
excerpt: You didn't reject me because I never asked. Sometimes, silence is the safest rejection.

title: The Mountain Air
excerpt: The high mountain air was freezing, but it was your polite, distant smile that truly gave me the chills.

title: The Ghosted Text
excerpt: You left me on 'read'. I convinced myself you were just too busy to reply.

title: The Accidental Touch
excerpt: Our shoulders brushed in the crowded elevator. I stopped breathing until the doors opened.

title: The Playlist
excerpt: I made a playlist of songs that reminded me of you, but I could never send you the link.

title: The Perfect Framerate
excerpt: I replayed the memory of your laugh at 60fps, smooth and perfect, a mental clip I could never delete.

title: The Observation
excerpt: I noticed you tap your fingers when you're nervous. I wonder if anyone else sees the little things like I do.

title: The Shift Change
excerpt: We were assigned different shifts. Suddenly, going to work felt like a punishment.

title: The Background Noise
excerpt: In the chaotic noise of the room, your voice was the only sound I could clearly tune into.

title: The Fake Smile
excerpt: I smiled when you introduced him to the group. It was the best acting performance of my life.

title: The Small Talk
excerpt: We talked about the weather. I wanted to talk about forever, but I settled for the forecast.

title: The Lost Keys
excerpt: I helped you look for your keys for thirty minutes, just happy to be useful to you.

title: The Inventory
excerpt: I counted the inventory twice, but I couldn't quantify the exact amount of times you crossed my mind.

title: The Scent
excerpt: Someone walked by wearing your perfume. I closed my eyes and pretended you were there.

title: The Shared Joke
excerpt: We had one inside joke. I held onto it like a lifeline in a sea of indifference.

title: The Wallpaper
excerpt: My phone wallpaper is a landscape, but I wish it could be the candid photo I took of you smiling.

title: The Hesitation
excerpt: I raised my hand to wave at you, then quickly pretended to fix my hair when you didn't look.

title: The Echoing Hall
excerpt: Your footsteps faded down the echoing hall, taking my temporary happiness with them.

title: The Morning Routine
excerpt: Even though my morning starts late, the very first thought in my mind is always your face.

title: The Empty Seat
excerpt: You were absent today. The chair next to me felt like a massive crater.

title: The Pretend Match
excerpt: I agreed with your completely wrong opinion just because I loved the way you argued.

title: The Distant Light
excerpt: You are the warm, glowing light at the end of the tunnel that I am not allowed to reach.

title: The Stolen Glances
excerpt: Looking at you when you aren't looking is my favorite, heartbreaking hobby.

title: The Blurry Background
excerpt: I focused on you so much that the rest of my life became a completely blurred background.

title: The Realization
excerpt: I realized today that I know you completely, and you barely know me at all.

title: The Fall
excerpt: Autumn leaves fell gracefully to the ground, much like my defenses whenever you spoke my name.

title: The Broken Promise
excerpt: I promised myself I would stop thinking about you. It's a promise I break every single day.

title: The Safe Distance
excerpt: I keep a safe distance so you don't feel uncomfortable, but the distance is slowly killing me.

title: The Comparison Game
excerpt: I compared myself to him and lost in every category that mattered to you.

title: The Long Drive
excerpt: I took a long drive alone, playing sad songs and pretending you were in the passenger seat.

title: The Hidden File
excerpt: Like a hidden file in a vast dataset, my love for you exists, but will never be processed.

title: The Small Details
excerpt: I notice the way you tie your shoes. I notice everything that you don't even think about.

title: The Quiet Room
excerpt: The room went quiet, and all I could hear was the deafening sound of my own unrequited feelings.

title: The Illusion
excerpt: For a second, I thought you were walking towards me. You were walking to the person behind me.

title: The Winter Scarf
excerpt: You wrapped your scarf tight. I wished I could be the one to keep you safe and warm.

title: The Social Post
excerpt: I refreshed your social media just to see if you looked happy. You did, without me.

title: The Unspoken Word
excerpt: There are a million words I want to say, but 'hello' is the only one I'm allowed to use.

title: The Shadow
excerpt: I am just a shadow in your bright, vibrant life. Present, but entirely unnoticeable.

title: The Good Morning
excerpt: Your casual 'good morning' gives me enough energy to survive the entire week.

title: The Final Hour
excerpt: At the end of the shift, I drag my feet, not wanting to leave the building where you are.

title: The Resignation
excerpt: I resigned myself to the fact that I will only ever be a supporting character in your story.

title: The Cold Truth
excerpt: The winter cold was harsh, but realizing you will never love me back was much colder.

title: The Perfect Match
excerpt: You are the perfect match to a puzzle I am not even allowed to touch.

title: The Silent Treatment
excerpt: You haven't spoken to me in days. You didn't do it on purpose, which makes it hurt more.

title: The Celebration
excerpt: I watched you celebrate your success. I clapped the loudest, completely invisible in the crowd.

title: The Holiday Party
excerpt: You looked stunning at the holiday party. I stayed in the corner holding a warm drink and a cold heart.

title: The Secret Wish
excerpt: I blew out my birthday candles and wished for you. It's the same wish I waste every single year.

title: The Afterthought
excerpt: I realized I am an afterthought to you, an 'oh yeah, him too' in the grand scheme of your life.

title: The Letting Go
excerpt: I told myself I would let you go before the year ends. I'm running out of time and courage.

title: The Last Look
excerpt: I looked at you one last time before leaving the room, memorizing the shape of your face.

title: The Acceptance
excerpt: I accepted that my heart chose you, even if yours will never, ever choose me back.

title: The Constant
excerpt: The year ends tonight, but my quiet, unbroken love for you will carry over into the next.

title: The Loop Begins
excerpt: Another year starts. I promised myself I would move on, but your smile immediately reset my progress.

title: The Enduring Silence
excerpt: Years may pass, but the silent, one-sided love story I carry for you will never need an audience.

title: The Final Frame
excerpt: You will always be the masterpiece I admire, and I will always be the artist who paints you from afar.
